message:
chore(beads): sync frankenterm issues.jsonl

Routine state sync from the beads tracker. Updates reflect issue
status transitions, comment additions, and assignee shuffles that
happened in the local SQLite store while the tree was being worked
on by parallel agents tonight.

The diff is exclusively in the existing tracker rows — no schema
changes, no deletions of issue history. Keeps the canonical
machine-readable form at .beads/issues.jsonl aligned with the local
SQLite store so other agents pulling main get the same view of the
backlog (in particular: which ft-* test-pinning beads are now closed
vs queued for the next round).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -76,7 +76,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-55l4f","title":"[review][MEDIUM] wa_wait_for.json golden freezes polls:1 — may flake under high-concurrency CI load","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_wait_for.json (landed in ecae6caa):

    "polls": 1

The canonicalize fn in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs only zeroes `now | elapsed_ms | captured_at | *_ms | score | semantic_score`. The `polls` field is not canonicalized.

Under load, this can flake:
- Each wa.wait_for poll spawns a fresh python3 subprocess (fake-wezterm.py) via tokio::Command in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:2142-2155.
- On first-poll cache-cold with the swarm running 20+ agents hammering CPU/disk, the subprocess spawn + file read can be slow enough that the poll scheduler issues a second poll before the first returns matching text (build ready matches ready$ immediately on content).
- polls jumps to 2+, golden is polls==1, test fails.

Repro likelihood: low under bench, moderate-to-high under concurrent cargo test + rch + other agent builds hammering I/O.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Canonicalize `polls` to 0 in canonicalize() alongside elapsed_ms/now/captured_at. Pro: stable golden. Con: loses a genuine signal — golden no longer catches regressions where wait_for suddenly needs 10 polls for a match it used to catch on the first try.
2. Split `polls` out of the golden capture: keep it in success_envelope but canonicalize to "polls >= 1" assertion in assert_wait_for_success_data (already there at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:~590 — `polls >= 1`), then canonicalize polls to 0 so the golden only sees the structural presence.
3. Accept the flake risk and document that re-running tests clears transient failures.

Option 2 is cleanest: the semantic "polls >= 1" invariant stays enforced; the byte-stable golden covers structure.

Severity: MEDIUM. Not a correctness bug; CI flake risk. Discovered during reviewer audit round 7, 2026-04-23 when verifying ecae6caa (ft-qyw1z+ft-4o5mb CRITICAL fix).

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, flake.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:30.203619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843379Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 05c8d36b (Option 2 from the bead). Semantic invariant polls>=1 remains enforced in assert_wait_for_success_data at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:474-477 (runs against the live envelope at :733, BEFORE canonicalization). canonicalize() at :480 now zeroes polls alongside now/elapsed_ms/captured_at so the byte-stable golden only freezes structural presence, not scheduler-timing-dependent count. wa_wait_for.json updated to polls:0 to match. Only golden in the tree carrying a polls key is wa_wait_for.json, so the canonicalize rule is unambiguous and cannot mask a real regression in another tool's envelope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
 {"id":"ft-5hp7p","title":"[config-types][test] extracted crate tests miss toml dev-dependency","description":"frankenterm-core-config-types inline tests use toml::to_string_pretty/from_str in tuning_config.rs, but the extracted crate manifest only declared serde. cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types failed with E0433 unresolved crate toml until the dev-dependency is restored.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:05.296081Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:40.437023Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:40.436733Z","close_reason":"Added the missing toml dev-dependency so cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types passes. Landed in 08e48279.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-5ikbd","title":"[conformance] robot-mode JSON envelopes: no runtime validator against docs/json-schema/","description":"## The gap

`docs/mcp-api-spec.md` states (verbatim):

> `data` MUST match the corresponding robot JSON schema under
> `docs/json-schema/`.

This is the central MUST clause of the MCP/robot surface. There is no
runtime test that enforces it.

## What exists today

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/schema_golden.rs` validates that:
  - Each `docs/json-schema/wa-robot-*.json` is well-formed JSON
  - Each registry entry maps to a file on disk
  - Each file has the required JSON-Schema metadata fields
  - `docs_gen` is deterministic
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/*.json` pins
  individual envelope *examples* but does not validate envelopes
  against the schema — it compares serialized output to a hand-crafted
  fixture.

## What's missing

- No `jsonschema` (or equivalent) crate in `Cargo.toml` workspace deps.
  `rg "jsonschema = " Cargo.toml crates/*/Cargo.toml` returns nothing.
- No test that takes a real `RobotResponse` / MCP tool envelope, runs
  it through `serde_json::to_value`, and validates against the
  corresponding `wa-robot-*.json` schema with a real Draft validator.
- The `additional_properties_leak` test in `schema_golden.rs` only
  checks the schemas themselves; a Rust type that adds a field but
  forgets to update the schema would land green.

## Why this catches real defects (skill criterion)

A `Robot*Response` struct gains a new `Option<u64>` field. The struct
serializes fine. `golden_robot_envelope/wa_state.json` tested a
specific case that doesn't exercise the new field, so it stays green.
`docs/json-schema/wa-robot-state.json` does NOT have the new field.
**Downstream JSON consumers (TypeScript clients, MCP agents) break.**
A schema-conformance harness catches this on the same commit that
adds the field.

This is exactly the differential / golden-file pattern the conformance
skill (Pattern 3) prescribes for serialization formats with an
authoritative spec.

## Suggested implementation

1. Add `jsonschema = "0.18"` (or equivalent) to dev-dependencies.
2. New test file `tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs`:
   - For each `(endpoint, schema_file)` pair from `SchemaRegistry`:
     - Deserialize the corresponding `golden_robot_envelope/*.json`
       fixture into the typed `Robot*Response`.
     - Re-serialize via `serde_json::to_value`.
     - Validate the serialized `Value` against the schema.
   - For each top-level production envelope returned by mcp_helpers,
     run a synthetic happy-path producer + validator.
3. Bonus: a coverage-matrix table at the bottom of the test that
   prints `(schema, validations_run, validations_passing)` so we can
   read it in CI and check that every shipped schema is reached.

## Out of scope

- Generating schemas FROM Rust types (that's `api_schema::SchemaRegistry`'s
  existing job; this bead validates the OTHER direction).
- Validating non-robot/MCP JSON outputs (separate beads if needed).

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test conformance_robot_envelope_schema`
  passes locally and in CI.
- Adding a field to a `Robot*Response` struct without updating the
  schema makes the test fail with a clear, fixture-tied error message.
- Test prints a coverage table: schemas exercised vs schemas in registry.

## References

- `docs/mcp-api-spec.md` (the MUST clause)
- `docs/json-schema/wa-robot-*.json` (the schemas)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/schema_golden.rs` (existing well-formedness gate)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/` (existing example fixtures)

Filed by cc_1 conformance audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:35:59.648425Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:35:59.648425Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 conformance audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:35:59.648425Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:56:47.770336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Zero ansi_byte_count after SIMD scan when flag is false; scan still runs to keep state.scan_state honest across chunks. Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.698143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.697874Z","close_reason":"Landed in commit 49937d56: storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing test pins the full StorageHandle reopen path. Asserts (1) fresh open with defer_fts_triggers=false installs all 3 output_segments_a[iud] triggers and synchronously indexes the first write, (2) reopen with defer_fts_triggers=false keeps all 3 triggers and synchronously indexes the second write via the trigger path. Combined with existing fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle (SQL-level true→false phase test) and fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip (5-iteration stress), the ft-ih4tm bidirectional contract is now pinned at both the connection and StorageHandle layers. Added Debug+Clone on StorageConfig for test ergonomics. All 3 tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5o6u5","title":"[security] Redactor leaves base64 secret suffixes unmasked","description":"Scope: redactor.rs secret handling.

Finding: GENERIC_API_KEY / GENERIC_TOKEN / GENERIC_SECRET only consume alnum/underscore/dash (and token dot) values, then Redactor::redact replaces only the regex match. Base64-style client secrets containing /, +, or = are partially redacted and leave trailing secret bytes visible.

Evidence: crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:47, crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:53, crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:65, crates/frankenterm-core/src/redactor.rs:220.

Repro unit:
let r = Redactor::new();
let input = "client_secret=abcdEFGHijklMNOP/QRST+UVWX=YZ1234567890";
let out = r.redact(input);
assert!(!out.contains("QRST+UVWX=YZ1234567890"));

Currently the assertion fails because the output leaves the /QRST+UVWX=YZ1234567890 suffix.

Impact: robot/MCP/audit paths that rely on Redactor can persist or return the unredacted suffix of common OAuth/client/base64 secrets.

Fix: extend generic secret/token/key value patterns to consume base64-ish quoted or whitespace-delimited values, and add regression coverage for /, +, and = values.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"amber-fox","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:37:13.774888Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:45:03.413816Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:45:03.413576Z","close_reason":"Extended GENERIC_API_KEY/GENERIC_TOKEN/GENERIC_SECRET value charsets to include base64 bytes /+= so OAuth/client_secret values are fully redacted. 5 regression tests added in redactor::tests covering all three patterns + whitespace-boundary preservation + detect() span. All pass via rch (frankenterm-core --lib redactor::). Commit 652600f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -527,7 +527,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-v5lz3.5","title":"[postswarm] Final wrap-up: verification commit + ROOT epic closure + retrospective notes","description":"# Final wrap-up — verification commit + ROOT closure

## Background
ROOT (ft-v5lz3) describes a session-wrap deliverable in its
"Definition of done" — "A summary commit lands documenting which
child closed which deliverable, and pointing at this bead." This
bead is that wrap-up. It runs LAST, after all sibling children
close.

## Why this matters
- Without a single wrap-up commit, the historical record of "what
  this epic accomplished" lives only in the bead descriptions.
  After enough sync churn the bead order in JSONL no longer
  reflects logical order; a wrap-up commit gives a single
  permanent anchor.
- A retrospective note captures lessons in the codebase, where
  future operators will find them via `git log --grep postswarm`.

## Scope
1. **Audit which children closed and what each delivered.**
   For each direct child of ROOT (cobalt-disposition, mem-gc-root,
   operator-tools-root, orch-lessons), list the close-reason and
   the commit(s) that landed under each.
2. **Compose a CHANGELOG-style entry** for the postswarm-2026-04-27
   work. Land it in:
   - docs/changelog.md (or similar) if the project has a changelog.
   - Otherwise: docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-summary.md.
3. **Compose a retrospective** capturing:
   - What worked well (the swarm shipped 35 commits + 16 closed
     beads in 1h45m).
   - What didn't (cobalt's silent abandonment of wa-nu4.3.9; the
     parent-child propagation bug; pane 3's zsh crash).
   - What we'd do differently next time (concrete process changes).
   Land at docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-retro.md.
4. **Make a single verification commit** with subject
   `chore(postswarm): close 2026-04-27 follow-up tracking epic
   (ft-v5lz3)`, listing every closed bead ID in the body.
5. **Close ROOT** with `br close ft-v5lz3 --reason "All children
   closed; retrospective at docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-retro.md;
   verification commit <SHA>"`.

## Out of scope
- Any new feature work or follow-on beads beyond what already
  exists in the postswarm tree.
- Editing AGENTS.md (that's orch-lessons' job).

## Approach
1. Run `br dep list ft-v5lz3` and capture the full child-tree.
2. For each child: `br show <id>` and read close-reason + closed-at.
3. For each child: `git log --since="2026-04-27" --grep <bead-id>
   --oneline` to find linked commits.
4. Compose the retrospective + summary + changelog entries.
5. Commit.
6. `br close ft-v5lz3`.
7. `br sync --flush-only` and final manual git add/commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-summary.md exists with a complete
  bead-id → commit-id mapping.
- docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-retro.md exists with what-worked /
  what-didn't / next-time sections.
- A single commit (subject `chore(postswarm)...`) lands and
  references all closed bead IDs.
- ROOT (ft-v5lz3) is in `closed` status.
- `bv --robot-insights` shows no new cycles introduced.
- `br ready` shows no remaining ready beads in the postswarm-
  2026-04-27 label set (unless they're stretch beads explicitly
  deferred).

## Testing
- The summary doc is verified by spot-checking 3 random closed
  bead IDs against `br show` output.
- The retrospective is read by a fresh agent who can summarize
  "what this swarm session accomplished" in one paragraph.
- Logging: this bead emits JSON-line events for each phase:
  {"phase":"audit-children","count":N},
  {"phase":"compose-summary","path":"<path>"},
  {"phase":"compose-retro","path":"<path>"},
  {"phase":"verify-commit","sha":"<SHA>"},
  {"phase":"close-root","reason":"..."}.

## Risks / considerations
- Don't close ROOT if any tier-1 child is still open. If
  ntm-coordinator integration (ft-v5lz3.2.5) or other stretch
  children remain open, close ROOT with a "deferred-stretches"
  note pointing at the still-open children OR re-parent them
  under a new "postswarm-2026-04-27 backlog" bead first.
- The retro must be honest. Don't whitewash the cobalt issue or
  the pane-3 crash; future operators benefit from accurate history.

## Dependencies (this bead is the LAST in the tree)
This bead depends on (is blocked by) all other tier-1 children
of ROOT: cobalt-disposition, mem-gc-root, operator-tools-root,
orch-lessons. It also depends on ft-ombfl closure (since ROOT is
related-to ft-ombfl).

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- wrap-up

## References
- ROOT epic ft-v5lz3.
- All siblings (cobalt-disp, mem-gc-root, ops-root, lessons).","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (failure recovery + commit organization)

### Commit message structure (organized by work-track, not flat)
With 30+ child beads, a flat list bloats the commit message. Use:
```
chore(postswarm): close 2026-04-27 follow-up tracking epic (ft-v5lz3)

Closes the postswarm-2026-04-27 tracking epic and its children.

Cobalt disposition (wa-nu4.3.9 audit + decomposition):
  - ft-v5lz3.4 (audit) — closed: <reason>
  - <list any audit-derived child beads>

Memory GC:
  - ft-v5lz3.1 (root) — closed
  - ft-v5lz3.1.1, .1.2, .1.3 (per-memory) — closed: <reason>
  - ft-v5lz3.1.4 (cron, stretch) — <closed | deferred>

Operator tooling:
  - ft-v5lz3.2 (root) — closed
  - ft-v5lz3.2.1..2.8 — closed: <each reason>
  - <stretches: list deferred>

Orchestration playbook:
  - ft-v5lz3.3 — landed in <SHA>: docs(agents): swarm orchestration playbook addendum

GPU regression harness:
  - ft-ombfl (root task) — closed
  - ft-ombfl.1..16 — closed: <each reason>
  - <stretches deferred: ft-ombfl.14, ft-ombfl.16>

See docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-summary.md for full mapping.
See docs/postswarm-2026-04-27-retro.md for retrospective.
```

### Failure-recovery procedures

**Pre-commit hook fails on the verification commit:**
- Diagnose the hook output; the project pre-commit guard (per
  AGENTS.md "Run bash scripts/install-hooks.sh") blocks mass
  deletions and frankenterm-core deletions.
- If the rejection is legitimate: the wrap-up commit is doing
  something it shouldn't. Fix and re-stage.
- If the rejection is spurious: NEVER use --no-verify (per
  AGENTS.md). File a bead to fix the hook properly.

**A child bead reveals it can't be closed (e.g., cobalt-disposition
audit shows the work is impossible without a separate engagement):**
- DO NOT close ROOT to "unblock" by force.
- Instead: file a NEW deferred bead capturing the unresolved scope
  and explicitly mark it as carried-over from postswarm-2026-04-27.
- Re-parent the deferred bead under a new "postswarm-2026-04-27
  carry-over" tracking task that lives independently of ROOT.
- Close ROOT noting the carry-over.

**A stretch bead has not been claimed before WRAPUP runs:**
- Stretch beads (P3/P4) are explicitly deferral-eligible.
- Move them to a new "postswarm-2026-04-27 backlog" tracking bead
  via `br update --parent <new-backlog-id>`.
- ROOT closes; the backlog stays open.

### Pre-WRAPUP checklist (mandatory before running this bead)
1. `br ready --json | jq -r '.[] | select(.id|startswith("ft-v5lz3")
   or startswith("ft-ombfl"))'` — confirm only stretch beads
   remain ready.
2. `git status` — confirm working tree is clean.
3. `cargo test --workspace` — confirm green.
4. `bash scripts/install-hooks.sh` was run (per AGENTS.md
   first-time-setup).
5. `bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'` returns [].

### Untracked-file cleanup (session artifacts)
The 2026-04-27 session left two untracked files at repo root:
`tmp.F6IsX749k7` and `tmp.IqA1dweVi5`. WRAPUP should:
- Inspect each: `cat tmp.F6IsX749k7 tmp.IqA1dweVi5 | head`.
- Decide: useful → move to scratch/ with a meaningful name; not
  useful → ASK before deleting (per AGENTS.md Rule 1, no file
  deletion without permission).

### Logging
Wrap-up emits a final session-end JSON-line burst:
```
{"phase":"wrap-up","action":"audit-children","count":N}
{"phase":"wrap-up","action":"compose-summary","path":"<path>"}
{"phase":"wrap-up","action":"compose-retro","path":"<path>"}
{"phase":"wrap-up","action":"verify-commit","sha":"<SHA>","files_changed":N}
{"phase":"wrap-up","action":"close-root","status":"closed"|"deferred-stretches","carry_over_bead":"<id>"}

-{"id":"ft-voeqm","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/recorder_migration_labruntime.rs: M0→M5 migration tests use MockMigrationStorage instead of real AppendLogRecorderStorage","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/recorder_migration_labruntime.rs (1063 lines)
defines two mocks for the migration target storage:
  153: struct MockMigrationStorage { ... }
  200: impl RecorderStorage for MockMigrationStorage
  272: struct MockCheckpointStorage { ... }
  306: impl RecorderStorage for MockCheckpointStorage
plus a TestEventReader (line 72) and TestCursor (line 82).

The tests cover the M0→M5 migration pipeline (the recorder schema/format
migration that moves recorded events from one backend to another).

## Why the mock is risky
Migration is the highest-risk path in the recorder: any drift between
mock semantics and real append-log semantics silently masks corruption
bugs. Specifically the mocks do NOT model:
- Append-log segment rotation boundaries
- fsync ordering vs checkpoint commits
- Offset monotonicity across crashes
- Queue-full backpressure at queue_capacity boundary
- Real RecorderStorageError propagation paths

A migration that passes every mock test can still corrupt production
recordings on first run because the mock returns success instantly while
the real backend has staged commit + journal-replay semantics.

Mock Risk score: 5 (data corruption) × 5 (mock ignores WAL/segment/
durability semantics) = 25 → MUST be mock-free.

## Real fixture available
crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs:612 defines
`AppendLogRecorderStorage` with an `open(config: AppendLogStorageConfig)`
constructor (line 673). It's the real on-disk backend the production
code uses. Pointing it at a per-test tempdir gives full real-storage
behavior with hermetic teardown.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `AppendLogStorageFixture` to tests/common/fixtures.rs that:
   - mktemp-d a per-test storage dir
   - opens AppendLogRecorderStorage::open against it
   - tears down via Drop (rm -rf)
2. Replace MockMigrationStorage with the fixture in the migration tests
   that probe data integrity (offsets, checkpoint commits, replay).
3. Keep MockCheckpointStorage only for tests that specifically inject
   fault paths (failed append, queue full) — those have different risk
   profile.
4. Add a regression test specifically for the corruption case the mock
   cannot catch: write 10k events, kill the storage handle mid-flush,
   reopen, verify offsets are monotonic and no events are lost OR
   torn.
5. JSON-line logging: emit per-phase events with bytes_written,
   segment_id, offset, checkpoint_commit_ms.

## Acceptance
- ≥4 migration tests use AppendLogRecorderStorage (not the mock).
- Crash-recovery regression test passes.
- Per-test wall time still < 200ms.
- No flake under cargo test --jobs 8.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:37.235730Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:37.235730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["migration","no-mocks","recorder","storage","test"]}

## Acceptance

- Crash-recovery regression test passes.
- Per-test wall time still < 200ms.
- No flake under cargo test --jobs 8.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:37.235730Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T08:57:14.941512Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["migration","no-mocks","recorder","storage","test"]}
 {"id":"ft-vue5t","title":"[security][P2][follow-up ft-x86z2] Gate wa.events_annotate/_triage/_label: thread Arc<Config> into their constructors","description":"## Context

ft-x86z2 closed 5 of the 8 ungated MCP mutators (wa.tx_run, wa.tx_rollback, wa.mission_abort/pause/resume) by adding a PolicyEngine.authorize gate before any side effect. Those structs already carry Arc<Config> so the gate helper could pick up operator-tuned safety settings.

Remaining 3 ungated tools — wa.events_annotate, wa.events_triage, wa.events_label — have struct fields `db_path: Arc<PathBuf>` and NO config. Adding the gate requires threading Arc<Config> into the constructor signature and updating call sites.

## Scope

- WaEventsAnnotateTool::new at mcp_tools.rs:4229
- WaEventsTriageTool::new at :4399
- WaEventsLabelTool::new at :4557
- Production call sites: mcp_bridge.rs:107, :112, :117 (add config arg)
- Test call sites: mcp_tools.rs:5195-5197 + 10+ per-tool test fixtures

Once each struct has `config: Arc<Config>`, insert the ft-x86z2 `mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation(self.config.as_ref(), "wa.events_annotate", "event.annotate", start)` gate after param validation, matching the pattern in tx_run/rollback/mission_*.

## Why P2, not P1

- The 5 more destructive operations (tx execute/rollback, mission state transitions) now ARE gated under ft-x86z2.
- events_annotate/triage/label write to event_notes/_triage_state/_labels, which are annotation-level and used in multi-agent coordination — worth gating but lower blast radius than tx/mission.
- Pure mechanical refactor; a dedicated pane can ship it cleanly without the ft-x86z2 time pressure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of wa.events_annotate/_triage/_label calls mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation before its mutation.
- Tests: per-tool deny-policy test asserts MCP_ERR_POLICY envelope instead of mutation.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while shipping ft-x86z2, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:11:31.181365Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:54.983328Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:54.982822Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 03eb5831: threaded Arc<Config> into wa.events_annotate/_triage/_label, added mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gates before mutation, and added MCP_ERR_POLICY deny tests. Verification partial: the narrow rch lane spent its time in full repo sync and did not reach a compile/test result in time.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-w36ut","title":"[reality-gap] AGENTS.md config snippet uses silently ignored or wrong-type workflow/pattern keys","description":"The operator-facing config snippet in AGENTS.md does not accurately describe the current parser behavior.

Evidence from the shipped binary:
- The documented `workflows.enabled = true` fails `ft config validate --path <snippet>` with `invalid type: boolean true, expected a sequence`.
- After changing only that one line to `enabled = []`, `ft config export --path ... --json` shows that `patterns.enabled_packs` is ignored; the effective config still uses `patterns.packs`.

Impact: AGENTS tells operators to use keys that either fail parse-time or validate while doing nothing.

Expected fix:
- Change `patterns.enabled_packs` to `patterns.packs`.
- Change `workflows.enabled = true` to a real list example or remove it from the snippet.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","close_reason":"Fixed by correcting the AGENTS.md snippet to `patterns.packs` and list-shaped workflow enablement.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"]}
